name: Snow Report 2026-02-22 05:51:54
report:
Good morning, everyone!

We hope you had a blast ripping up the creamy snow that was covering the mountain yesterday. After 12” in less than 24 hours our base depth is looking really solid and snowmaking even kept rolling through the storm to boost things further. All this snow has our options wide open and we’re continuing our streak of leading the East with 100% of our trails ready to shred. 

Light snow showers are possible this morning, followed by mostly cloudy skies and a high near 27° at the base and 21° at the peak. An east wind at 15–17 mph will keep it feeling crisp as we’ve got another classic mid-winter day lined up. 

Our key lifts are scheduled to spin starting at 8:00 a.m., with the exception of the Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad starting at 9:00 a.m.

The snow surfaces on the slopes yesterday were deep and supportive, with depths hanging around for much of the morning in spots and transitioning into creamy packed powder in others. The edges were grabbing amazingly and made for confident turns. This more supportive snow will help build up our base layer, making every inch count later in the season. The South Ridge was a blast to rip once it opened for business and classics like Cruise Control provided fun turns all over. The glades obviously had goods as well, with the Stash Woods offering a creative playground covered in pow.  We can expect a lot of sweet, packed powder on natural terrain today with untracked powder still waiting in certain spots if you know where to look. 

Our grooming team added some favorites to the quiver in Escapade, Breakaway, Upper Wildfire, and Ovation. Groomed trails will have more speed than the ones covered in snow recently and will be perfect for carving on machine groomed packed powder. Head over to Bear Mountain for a wide range of smooth trails to choose from with Bear Claw as one of my favorites to shred down. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report to see the whole list and plan out your arcing sessions. 

Our snowmakers are doing their best to work with unfavorable Easterly winds and fired up the snowguns on Superstar and Skyelark. They also ran guns on Great Northern and Bear Trax from the top of South Ridge down to Skyewalker just for the night. This snowmaking run should last all the way through Wednesday, bringing even more cover to trails we hope to see skiing well into the spring. 

Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. 

The Timberline halfpipe will be closed to the public today during the USASA event. The upper section of Timberline will remain open. Non-competitors can exit above the halfpipe via Easy Street and can re-enter Timberline at the Header entrance about halfway down the trail.  

The USA vs. Canada gold medal hockey game will be playing at the K-1 Base Lodge, the Peak Lodge, and the Long Trail Pub on the top floor of the Snowshed Lodge at 8:10 a.m. this morning. 

Let’s have a fun Sunday!

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 103 

Lifts    

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.   

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.    

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!    

Uphill Travel    

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route. 

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-21 09:08:25
report:
Morning, Beast fans! 

Yesterday turned into a full-blown blizzard, with snow stacking up through the night. The mountain picked up another 9 inches under the cover of darkness, bringing this storm total to a lofty 12 inches!

Our Mountain Ops teams came out in force this morning to get the mountain open early for our powder day, and we currently have all of our lifts open at this time! Big thanks to our Lift Operations crew and many others—the Bear Mountain Quad and Snowdon Six both opened by 7:30 a.m. for early laps, and all other lifts came online by 9:00 a.m.

Today’s weather looks like we’re in for another comfy day, with base temps hovering in the mid 20s all day. Winds will be very gentle at the bottoms of lifts and won’t gust higher than around 25mph at the peak. Seems like the bulk of the snow from this storm will fall before the morning, although intermittent snow showers may continue until noon. Please be cautious when commuting up to the mountain this morning; our parking attendants will be out in force to make sure we can use our lots as efficiently as possible.

As for the snow conditions, not much more to say besides that we’re in for a real powder day. Before the snow came down, groomed runs were running ultra smooth, with a couple highlights being Lower Pipe Dream and Lower Wildfire. Today, truly everything will be in play, as we’re still boasting 100% of our open terrain. Powder day classics like Helter Skelter will get plenty of love, while glade runs like Twister and Roundabout Glade are sure to be a blast.

On the grooming front, our crew tackled an audacious 89 trails, with many of the usuals making the cut plus a few additions. Solitude and Sassafras got a grooming pass, so making first tracks on those winding trails will be a bit easier. All of Low Road is smoothed out, giving an extra groomed option into Snowshed from Skye Peak. Frolic, Home Stretch, Pipe Dream, and many more were also nice and smooth by this morning—check our Lifts & Trails Report for the whole list.

As if this storm wasn’t bringing enough snow, we’re back to making our own. That’s right: we’ll be turning the snowguns on this afternoon on Superstar, Skyelark, Bittersweet, High Road, and additional connecting trails around the mountain. This snowmaking run should last all the way through Wednesday, bringing even more cover to trails we hope to see skiing well into the spring. 

Shout out to our kparks team, who had a tall task in cleaning out each of our 6 open parks this morning. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. 

Enjoy the pow!

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-21 05:46:16
report:
Morning, Beast fans! 

Yesterday turned into a full-blown blizzard, with snow stacking up through the night. The mountain picked up another 9 inches under the cover of darkness, bringing this storm total to a lofty 12 inches!

If you want to be the first to the powder, we have great news for you: the Bear Mountain Quad is scheduled to start spinning at 7:30 a.m. today. The rest of our key lifts are due to spin starting at 8:00 a.m., with the exception of Canyon Quad, South Ridge Quad, and the Snowdon Triple starting at 9:00 a.m. and the Snowshed Double starting at 10:00 a.m. 

Today’s weather looks like we’re in for another comfy day, with base temps hovering in the mid 20s all day. Winds will be very gentle at the bottoms of lifts and won’t gust higher than around 25mph at the peak. Seems like the bulk of the snow from this storm will fall before the morning, although snow showers may continue until noon. Please be cautious when commuting up to the mountain and our parking attendants will be out in force to make sure we can use our lots as efficiently as possible.

As for the snow conditions, not much more to say besides that we’re in for a real powder day. Before the snow came down, groomed runs were running ultra smooth, with a couple highlights being Lower Pipe Dream and Lower Wildfire. Today, truly everything will be in play, as we’re still boasting 100% of our open terrain. Powder day classics like Helter Skelter will get plenty of love, while glade runs like Twister and Roundabout Glade are sure to be a blast. 

On the grooming front, our crew tackled an audacious 89 trails, with many of the usuals making the cut plus a few additions. Solitude and Sassafras got a grooming pass, so making first tracks on those winding trails will be a bit easier. All of Low Road is smoothed out, giving an extra groomed option into Snowshed from Skye Peak. Frolic, Home Stretch, Pipe Dream, and many more will also be nice and smooth—check our Lifts & Trails Report for the whole list.

As if this storm wasn’t bringing enough snow, we’re back to making our own. That’s right: we’ll be turning the snowguns on this afternoon on Superstar, Skyelark, Bittersweet, High Road, and additional connecting trails around the mountain. This snowmaking run should last all the way through Wednesday, bringing even more cover to trails we hope to see skiing well into the spring. 

Shout out to our kparks team, a few of whom are up in Burlington to build & maintain the course for this year’s Burton Winter Jam. In the meantime, the lineup in our own Killington Parks stays stacked. Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. 

Enjoy the pow!

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-20 16:29:30
report:
Good morning, Killington,

It was another unreal one at The Beast yesterday, with bright, clear skies setting the stage for buttery turns across the mountain. And we’ve got more good news on the way. Snow moves in this afternoon, with flakes picking up after 2 p.m. under breezy east winds and temps topping out around 26 degrees at the summit and 29 degrees at the base. We’re tracking 8 to 12 inches of fresh snow between this afternoon and Saturday night. Fresh turns are definitely on the menu!

We're still 100% open and are serving up the most expansive playground in the East. From top-to-bottom groomers to bump lines and hidden tree shots, it’s all in play. Snow surfaces were gripping beautifully again, letting you link one deep turn after another as the sun and warmer temps kept the slopes soft, smooth, and playful. Groomed trails provide confidence inducing turns and are a blast to lean the edges into. Even as the day goes on, the smooth runs that turn into soft packed and packed powder continue to have a fun surface to rip on. 

Our groomers went after the same program except they left Ovation on natural. Downdraft saw a resurface again and Superstar is still skiing & riding really well. Make your way over to Needle’s Eye on Cruise Control for a classic, cruise worthy run that never disappoints. For our full list of groomers, check out the Lifts & Trails Report. 

Snowmaking operations are still on hold for the time being due to warmer temps, but we could see the snowguns fire up later this week. If you’re curious to learn more about snowmaking and the natural challenges our crew has faced this winter, take a look at our most recent Insider Blog. 

Lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the South Ridge Quad and Canyon Quad opening soon after at 10:00 a.m. The Bear Mountain Quad will also spin again, offering full access to Outer Limits and Devil’s Fiddle, amongst other favorites. 

Over in kparks, the lineup stays stacked. Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, and The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options.

Today, we'll be hosting 200K Shred Day to celebrate Killington hitting 200,000 followers on Instagram! Join us for our all-day party, first featuring a scavenger hunt for 20 YETI mugs hidden around the mountain. Find one to keep, and you’ll score a 1-day lift ticket voucher, valid through June 1, 2027. We'll also be leading a party lap from the top of Superstar at 2:20 p.m., immediately followed by an après scene at the bottom of Superstar featuring DJ Stevie B and $2.20 Michelob ULTRA, plus complimentary NA beverages and giveaways.

Finally, our Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open daily this week from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. 

Get fired up for a fun Friday! 

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-20 13:55:04
report:
Good morning, Killington,

It was another unreal one at The Beast yesterday, with bright, clear skies setting the stage for buttery turns across the mountain. And we’ve got more good news on the way. Snow moves in this afternoon, with flakes picking up after 2 p.m. under breezy east winds and temps topping out around 26 degrees at the summit and 29 degrees at the base. We’re tracking 8 to 12 inches of fresh snow between this afternoon and Saturday night. Fresh turns are definitely on the menu!

We're still 100% open and are serving up the most expansive playground in the East. From top-to-bottom groomers to bump lines and hidden tree shots, it’s all in play. Snow surfaces were gripping beautifully again, letting you link one deep turn after another as the sun and warmer temps kept the slopes soft, smooth, and playful. Groomed trails provide confidence inducing turns and are a blast to lean the edges into. Even as the day goes on, the smooth runs that turn into soft packed and packed powder continue to have a fun surface to rip on. 

Our groomers went after the same program except they left Ovation on natural. Downdraft saw a resurface again and Superstar is still skiing & riding really well. Make your way over to Needle’s Eye on Cruise Control for a classic, cruise worthy run that never disappoints. For our full list of groomers, check out the Lifts & Trails Report. 

Snowmaking operations are still on hold for the time being due to warmer temps, but we could see the snowguns fire up later this week. If you’re curious to learn more about snowmaking and the natural challenges our crew has faced this winter, take a look at our most recent Insider Blog. 

Lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the South Ridge Quad and Canyon Quad opening soon after at 10:00 a.m. The Bear Mountain Quad will also spin again, offering full access to Outer Limits and Devil’s Fiddle, amongst other favorites. 

Over in kparks, the lineup stays stacked. Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, and The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options.

Today, we'll be hosting 200K Shred Day to celebrate Killington hitting 200,000 followers on Instagram! Join us for our all-day party, first featuring a scavenger hunt for 20 YETI mugs hidden around the mountain. Find one to keep, and you’ll score a 1-day lift ticket voucher, valid through June 1, 2027. We'll also be leading a party lap from the top of Superstar at 2:20 p.m., immediately followed by an après scene at the bottom of Superstar featuring DJ Stevie B and $2.20 Michelob ULTRA, plus complimentary NA beverages and giveaways.

Finally, our Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open daily this week from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. 

Get fired up for a fun Friday! 

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 101 

Lifts   

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.  

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.   

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!

All uphill travel routes are currently open. Please see our About Killington & Pico Uphill Travel page for specific route information.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-19 05:48:14
report:
Happy 100th day, Beast fans!

The conditions were absolutely beautiful yesterday, with soft snow that made it easy to dig your edges in and sunshine that added to the fun. We’ll have more of those great surfaces waiting for you today, with 100% of our trails open and the most terrain offered in the East. We're also under a storm watch with 5 to 9 inches of new snow possible staring in the early afternoon tomorrow through Saturday.

Mostly sunny skies are on tap, with a high near 29° at the base and 24° at the peak. Light winds out of the northeast will shift southwest in the afternoon, keeping things comfortable as you chase the sun across The Beast.

Pretty much everything was fun to shred yesterday as the recent warmer temps softened up the snow and made for ideal surfaces to turn and slide on. Rime was the spot for silky smooth corduroy and everything at Bear Mountain has been shredding fantastically. 

Our grooming team worked through the night resurfacing over 80 trails. They threw Lower Downdraft and Middle Ovation on the plan which are both perfect runs for those chasing an adrenaline rush of carving. Superstar also has fresh corduroy and has been fun to ride. Head over to Bunny Buster for something more mellow that still has prime surfaces for arcing in the morning. Groomed surfaces will turn to equally enjoyable soft pack and packed powder as the day goes on. For our full list of groomers, check out the Lifts & Trails Report. 

Snowmaking operations are still on hold for the time being due to warmer temps, but we could see the snowguns fire back up later on this week. If you’re curious to learn more about snowmaking and the natural challenges our crew has faced this winter, take a look at our most recent Insider Blog. 

Lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the South Ridge Quad and Canyon Quad opening soon after at 10:00 a.m. The Bear Mountain Quad will also spin again, offering full access to Outer Limits and Devil’s Fiddle, amongst other black diamond favorites. 

Over in kparks, the lineup stays stacked. Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, and The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options.

Tomorrow, February 20th, we'll be hosting 200K Shred Day to celebrate Killington hitting 200,000 followers on Instagram! Join us for our all-day party, first featuring a scavenger hunt for 20 YETI mugs hidden around the mountain. Find one to keep, and you’ll score a 1-day lift ticket voucher, valid through June 1, 2027. We'll also be leading a party lap from the top of Superstar at 2:20 p.m., immediately followed by an après scene at the bottom of Superstar featuring a live DJ and $2.20 Michelob ULTRA, plus complimentary NA beverages and giveaways.

Finally, our Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open daily this week from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. 

More great shredding and fun scenes await! 

– Evan
